verses.text: At Thessalonica Paul reasons from the scriptures 'that Christ must needs have suffered, and risen.' From the word. He proves the gospel out of Scripture.
At Thessalonica Paul reasons from the scriptures 'that Christ must needs have suffered, and risen.' From the word. He proves the gospel out of Scripture.
At Thessalonica Paul reasons from the scriptures 'that Christ must needs have suffered, and risen.' From the word. He proves the gospel out of Scripture.
Some believe, but the envious Jews set the city in an uproar. Stirred envy. Jealousy fights the truth it cannot answer.
Some believe, but the envious Jews set the city in an uproar. Stirred envy. Jealousy fights the truth it cannot answer.
They drag Jason, 'these that have turned the world upside down are come hither.' Unwitting praise. The gospel's power is owned even by its foes.
They drag Jason, 'these that have turned the world upside down are come hither.' Unwitting praise. The gospel's power is owned even by its foes.
They drag Jason, 'these that have turned the world upside down are come hither.' Unwitting praise. The gospel's power is owned even by its foes.
They drag Jason, 'these that have turned the world upside down are come hither.' Unwitting praise. The gospel's power is owned even by its foes.
At Berea they 'searched the scriptures daily'; many believe. Noble hearers. They test the message by the word of God.
At Berea they 'searched the scriptures daily'; many believe. Noble hearers. They test the message by the word of God.
At Berea they 'searched the scriptures daily'; many believe. Noble hearers. They test the message by the word of God.
Stirred up again, Paul is sent to Athens. Driven on. Opposition spreads the gospel to new cities.
Stirred up again, Paul is sent to Athens. Driven on. Opposition spreads the gospel to new cities.
Stirred up again, Paul is sent to Athens. Driven on. Opposition spreads the gospel to new cities.
At Athens his spirit is stirred by the idols; he disputes in the market. Grieved by idolatry. He cannot be silent where God is dishonoured.
At Athens his spirit is stirred by the idols; he disputes in the market. Grieved by idolatry. He cannot be silent where God is dishonoured.
At Athens his spirit is stirred by the idols; he disputes in the market. Grieved by idolatry. He cannot be silent where God is dishonoured.
They bring him to Areopagus to hear 'this new doctrine,' loving novelty. Curious crowd. They seek new ideas more than truth.
They bring him to Areopagus to hear 'this new doctrine,' loving novelty. Curious crowd. They seek new ideas more than truth.
They bring him to Areopagus to hear 'this new doctrine,' loving novelty. Curious crowd. They seek new ideas more than truth.
Ye are too superstitious; I found an altar, 'TO THE UNKNOWN GOD'; 'him declare I unto you.' The true God. Paul makes known the God they ignorantly own.
Ye are too superstitious; I found an altar, 'TO THE UNKNOWN GOD'; 'him declare I unto you.' The true God. Paul makes known the God they ignorantly own.
God made the world; He 'dwelleth not in temples,' nor is served by men's hands, but gives life to all. Self-sufficient. The Maker needs nothing from us.
God made the world; He 'dwelleth not in temples,' nor is served by men's hands, but gives life to all. Self-sufficient. The Maker needs nothing from us.
He made all nations of one blood, that they 'should seek the Lord... though he be not far.' Near to all. God set men to seek and find Him.
He made all nations of one blood, that they 'should seek the Lord... though he be not far.' Near to all. God set men to seek and find Him.
'In him we live, and move, and have our being'; the Godhead is not like gold or stone. Living God. He is no lifeless image of man's making.
'In him we live, and move, and have our being'; the Godhead is not like gold or stone. Living God. He is no lifeless image of man's making.
God 'commandeth all men every where to repent,' having appointed a day to judge by the risen man. Universal call. Repentance is required of all before the judgment.
God 'commandeth all men every where to repent,' having appointed a day to judge by the risen man. Universal call. Repentance is required of all before the judgment.
Some mock the resurrection, some believe; Dionysius and Damaris among them. Divided hearing. The same word draws scorn and faith.
Some mock the resurrection, some believe; Dionysius and Damaris among them. Divided hearing. The same word draws scorn and faith.
